characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Actor Allusion:Missy Peregrymlast played a law enforcement officer inRookie Blue, but this time she's got moreLEOexperience. Brainy Brunette: She has brown hair. Crusading Widow:In season 1 once she realises that her late husband's death was not actually an accident. Fair Cop: One of many pretty FBI agents assigned to New York. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: The responsible sibling to her drug addict sister Erin's foolish.Subverted in later seasons when Maggie is convinced that a now-clean Erin has relapsed when Erin has actually remained clean, causing a rift between the sisters as Maggie realises that she needs to stay away from Erin as no matter the progress her sister makes, Maggie is unable to trust in her sobriety due to Erin's past.

characters.name: Maggie Bell
characters.description: Actor Allusion:Missy Peregrymlast played a law enforcement officer inRookie Blue, but this time she's got moreLEOexperience. Brainy Brunette: She has brown hair. Crusading Widow:In season 1 once she realises that her late husband's death was not actually an accident. Fair Cop: One of many pretty FBI agents assigned to New York. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: The responsible sibling to her drug addict sister Erin's foolish.Subverted in later seasons when Maggie is convinced that a now-clean Erin has relapsed when Erin has actually remained clean, causing a rift between the sisters as Maggie realises that she needs to stay away from Erin as no matter the progress her sister makes, Maggie is unable to trust in her sobriety due to Erin's past.

characters.name: OA Zidan
characters.description: Badges and Dog Tags: An ex-US Army officer with the rank of Captain prior to joining the FBI. Berserk Button: As a devout Muslim, stereotypicalMiddle Eastern Terroristsbring out his unforgiving side.Terrorist: I'm doing Allah's work.Zidan:People like you shouldn't get to say Allah's name!Also,if his little sister is in danger... Also,if his little sister is in danger... Culture Clash: While partnered up withthe visiting Detective Hailey Upton, the two of them get in a little argument about each other's home city, with OA noting the bad publicity of Chicago's police force and Upton snarking about New York pizza being too much "like a cracker with sauce on it." They eventually end up on good terms. Disappeared Dad: His father is deceased.

characters.name: Jubal Valentine
characters.description: Amicable Exes: When his ex-wife Samantha tells him she wants to move to Westchester County with the kids, he is reluctant at first, but agrees after checking out the neighborhood. Family Business: In season 5, both Jubal and his ex-wife are taken aback when their son Tyler shows interest in becoming an FBI agent. NO INDOOR VOICE: He raises his voice alot— often because he's addressing a group, but sometimes even when he doesn't really need to. Number Two: Second-in-command of the New York field office. Office Romance: Dates FBI Assistant Director Rina Trenholm until her untimely death.

characters.name: Isobel Castille
characters.description: Early-Bird Cameo: She makes a brief appearance in the Season OnePoorly Disguised PilotforFBI: Most Wantedbefore replacing Dana as a main character. Happily Married: Reveals in season 7 that she got married 2 months previously to a man called Phillip and that she now has 3 stepdaughters. Mama Bear: If any of her agents are in danger, she won't rest until she manages to get them out of their safely.Deconstructedin that her desire to protect them and stop theUnSub's can cause her to get erratic, making very hasty decisions, and lose what moral scruples she had left. Nice Job Breaking It, Hero!: In Isobel's desperation to arrest Vargas and stop the bombs he planted, she sends FBI agents to locate his wife and son who were in hiding, then threatens Vargas with saying they'll go after them if he doesn't surrender. Once he did, she orders her men to leave without a second thought. This leaves his wife and son defenseless when some of Vargas' enemies from a rival cartel, having taken notice of the FBI's presence, kidnap and kill them via hanging from a bridge. Vargas blames not just Isobel, but the FBI in general, for their deaths since they were only killed because Isobel exposed their location and left without care for making sure no one else found them, sending him on aRoaring Rampage of Revengeeven from behind bars, causing him to begin targeting not just the FBI agents, buttheirfamilies too,successfully killing Rina Trenholm. Even after they stop his men though, Jubal acknowledges that Vargas likely isn't done, and if they don't take care of himnow, he'llneverstop until they're all dead. Jubal eventually takes action in "FBI: International" with the Fly Team. Parental Issues: In Season 4's "Scar Tissue", Isobel is seen to have a fractious estranged relationship with her father due to his failure to show up for her mother's funeral. Isobel's father revealed that her mother had asked him not to and that he kept his promise even at the cost of damaging his relationship wi

characters.name: Stuart Scola
characters.description: Action Dad: By the sixth season, Stuart's been trying to do his best to juggle his FBI work while trying to be a father. Badges and Dog Tags: Zig-zagged. Stuart was a military academy graduate from Cornwall Academy. Casting Gag: This isn'tthe first timeJohn Boydportrayed an FBI agent who has a complicated history with the financial sector. Dark and Troubled Past: His brother died in the World Trade Center on 9/11. He initially claims he was lying, but later admits to Kristen that he wasn't. Freudian Excuse: Admits to OA in “Money For Nothing” that his open disdain for high rollers who obsess with presenting themselves as successful and enjoying the high life regardless of their actual financial situation stems from his childhood as his father was one. Posing as a successful high-flying property relator, in truth he wasliving vastly beyond his means and his entire image was just a house of cards. Stuart admits whilst they got to enjoy a high standard of living, he was disillusioned to realise his father cared more about presenting himself as a success than actually providing for his family, and the result was that he had virtually nothing and was in severe debt by the time he died, yet still carried on living like he was a millionaire up till the end.

characters.name: Dana Mosier
characters.description: Benevolent Boss: She looks out for her subordinates. Put on a Bus: Submits her retirement papers in the season 1 finale after making a work decision based on feelings; letting Maggie keep working on a murder case that also involves the murder of Maggie's late husband. If the case had ended badly, it could have ended Maggie's career or worse. The Profiler: She was one in her previous role, and often gets to use her old skills. Reasonable Authority Figure Second Episode Substitute: She replaces the previous boss after the pilot.

characters.name: Kristen Chazal
characters.description: Rank Up: Works as a technical analyst in season 1 before becoming a field agent in season 2. Put on a Bus: It is revealed in the premier episode of season 3 that Kristen has moved to Texas and joined the FBI field office there. Slashed Throat: Suffers one in season 2 when making an arrest though eventually makes a full recovery. The Smart Girl: The technical type, she's one of the FBI's residentTechno Wizards.

characters.name: Nina Chase
characters.description: Action Mom: Goes out into the field after her son's birth. Dating What Daddy Hates: Her father might not hate Stuart, but he does sneeringly claim Stuart is showing off by paying for things with his 'fancy black credit card' and clearly and critically feels that Stuart comes from money, while Nina and her family apparently do not. Embarrassing First Name: Her father keeps calling her Marina, which is apparently her birth name and one that Nina clearly doesn't like as she corrects her father that she prefers to be called Nina. The reason for her dislike of her birth name is currently unknown. Fair Cop: A very attractive woman with high cheekbones like a model, certainly helps that she's played byShantel VanSanten. Happily Married: Averted and later invoked with her and Scola (in fact, she even stated that marriage won't be happening anytime soon for them), but they are happily together. They finally tie the knot inConfetti.

characters.name: Tiffany Wallace
characters.description: Big Brother Instinct: A Big Sister version. Tiffany has to persuade her troubled younger brother to get medical help when his behaviour becomes increasingly erratic. Fair Cop: Spent six years with the New York Police Department before becoming an FBI Agent. My Greatest Failure: While working as a patrol officer, Tiffany was forced to shoot a mentally-unstable man in self-defense. Years later, the shooting would come back to haunt her when she encounters the man's son Jamal who was sent into foster care and ended up with a gang of bank robbers.Jamal: "Wish you could ever just start the day all over again and pick completely different choices?"Tiffany: "Yeah. More than you'll ever know."Also Trevor Hobbs's death in 'All The Rage' in Season 6 as she sent him to watch a suspect who then killed Hobbs. It becomes Tiffany's obsession throughout that season to find and bring down Hobbs's murderer, something she achieves in the season finale. Also Trevor Hobbs's death in 'All The Rage' in Season 6 as she sent him to watch a suspect who then killed Hobbs. It becomes Tiffany's obsession throughout that season to find and bring down Hobbs's murderer, something she achieves in the season finale. Put on a Bus: Leaves New York after the first episode of Season 7. She tells Maggie (and OA) that she's going to Georgia to work with her sister.
